_doc: Example MCP server registry. Copy to ~/.arbiter/mcp_servers.json and
edit to taste. Arbiter speaks stdio MCP only, so each entry below uses
`mcp-remote` (npm) as a stdio<->HTTP bridge for the hosted services.
Requires Node.js + npx on the host running `arbiter --api`.
OAuth: Sentry, Linear, and Slack trigger an OAuth flow on first
connect — mcp-remote prints a login URL to stderr and waits. Pre-
authenticate from a shell once (`npx mcp-remote <url>` and complete
the browser flow) so the token caches at ~/.mcp-auth/ before
GitHub: api.githubcopilot.com requires a Copilot subscription. If
you don't have Copilot, swap the entry for the stdio package:
and export GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN in the launcher's environment.
Arbiter ignores top-level keys other than `servers`, so this _doc
block is harmless and survives JSON parse.
